calendar.matters:
Petition To Amend Order For Final Distribution, Settling First And Final Account, And Allowing Compensation For Ordinary And Extraordinary Services And For Reimbursement Of Costs
Off Calendar Feb-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Actions
Off Calendar Jul-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300066 Estate of Willie Lee Hughes and PES-17-300905 Estate of Lauretta E.B. Hughes.

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by telephone for Lauretta Hughes.
Donna Quan and Christy Styer appeared by telephone for Sean Hughes.
Jessica Rowen-Perl appeared by telephone for Maichael Valencia.

The Court stated that it is not that concerned that the request for Court retention under CCP 664.6 was not identified in the petition, that the Parties can't agree on fees, and that statutory and extraordinary fees and costs have to be reviewed and approved by the Court. The Court also asked the Parties if they agree that their fee agreement is not binding on the Court. Ms. Quan stated that she has no problem with that and that it is her understanding that fees at the discretion of the Court. Ms. Perl stated that she is okay with it and that she does not object to the accounting as amended. Mr. Worth stated that he has no objection. The Court noted that it can't approve final distribution because they still have the issues of fees. Ms. Quan stated that the petition does ask for statutory fees, extraordinary fees and costs. The Court stated that the fee request from Sean seems a little high. Ms. Qaun stated that this was an eight-year administration, that the work involved tax issues, sale of property, and that as indicated in Mr. Bassi's declaration the statutory fees are inadequate to compensate for the amount of extraordinary work that was required for this administration. Ms. Quan also stated that they had to go back to the date of death to quantify the amount of rent that was calculated and that the accounting began at date of death, not with the date of appointment. The Court noted that the Petition for Preliminary Distribution, etc. is taken off calendar by Ms. Perl. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement of Actions, and the Amended Petition for Order Approving Settlement of Actions (subject to review of the orders). The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, Petition for Settlement and Allowance of Statutory and Extraordinary Fees, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.
